subject: Mozi Minute: 70% Calendar Rule of Thumb
body:
Words I like: You can't beat someone who has already decided they will do whatever it takes for as long as it takes. And that person might as well be you.

Mozi Minute: The 70% Calendar Rule of Thumb

Most sales managers (& entrepreneurs) celebrate when calendars are packed.

I've found that's not actually the sweet spot for maximum sales efficiency.

I've found that to be closer to 70%.

100% Scheduled = Lower Conversion
When reps are fully booked... They don't have time for follow-up, they can't squeeze the pipeline, and they rush calls.

30% Scheduled = Commission Breath
When reps have too few appointments... They get desperate, they try to close too hard or too early.

70% Scheduled = Maximum Output
This is just below the comfort point for most salespeople. It forces them to work their pipeline. They have time for consultations and follow-up meetings. But they're not so empty they get desperate.

What To Do:
If you're consistently at 80%+ utilization, you probably need more salespeople.

Hypothetical Math:
5 reps at 100% utilization closing 20% = 100 deals
7 reps at 70% utilization closing 30% = 147 deals

More reps at lower utilization can close more deals than fewer reps at full capacity.

Talk soon,
- Alex
